items.question: What should I know before studying RT-X?
items.answer: Use the broader Neural Networks context, the overview block, and the comparison view to orient yourself. Ask whether the term changes layers, activations, gradients, optimization, or training dynamics.

items.question: What should I compare RT-X with?
items.answer: Compare it with RT-2 and RT-1 before moving on to RT-1, RT-2 (Robotics Transformer Models) and RT-X benchmark.

blocks.title: Quick Check
blocks.question: How should you place RT-X in the learning graph?
options: Treat it as an isolated fact
Jump only to RT-1, RT-2 (Robotics Transformer Models)
blocks.explanation: RT-X is easiest to understand by comparing it with RT-2, since neighbors expose overlap, difference, and boundary conditions.
